violations.text: Management's plan for assessing, monitoring, and controlling foodborne disease hazards is inadequate. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/23/2026

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/23/2026
Inspector observed staff touching raw meat with gloved hands and then touching burger bun with same gloved hands. PIC disposed of bun. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/23/2026

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC disposed of ice and turned off unit for washing/sanitizing. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/23/2026

Cut tomatoes were observed held at 54 degrees Fahrenheit in the small prep unit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC added more ice for active cooling. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/23/2026

The knives are stored on an unclean surface (in the crevice of the prep unit hood) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. PIC removed. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/23/2026

The following was observed in poor repair: - small prep unit (nonfunctional) - staff are using ice to keep items cool. - dshwasher located in the kitchen area was not in good repair (does not get hot enough to sanitize). Per interview with PIC, establishment is using dishwasher to wash and 3 bay sink to sanitize. New Violation. Correct By: 05/07/2026
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the quat AND chlorine sanitizing solution for the 3 bay sinks. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the (walk-in fan blades and microwave shelf) have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 04/23/2026
The liquids from the keg machines and soda dispensing gun is draining into containers. Nonsewage liquids must drain into a drain. New Violation. Correct By: 04/23/2026
The receptacle in the women's toilet room is lacking a cover. A toilet room used by females shall be provided with a covered receptacle for sanitary napkins. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2024
The floor in the kitchen area is in poor repair (cracked tiles). The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 10/31/2026

The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. Management's plan for assessing, monitoring, and controlling foodborne disease hazards is inadequate. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2026

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2026
Handwashing sink at the bar was observed being used as a dump sink. Staff removed strainer from sink. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2026
The hand-washing sink in the bathroom and bar were lacking proper handwashing signage. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2024

The food-contact surfaces of equipment and utensils were not sanitized. The required utensil surface temperature of 160 degrees Fahrenheit was not achieved during the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher as evidenced by the failure of the thermolabel (temperature indicator) affixed to the surface of a utensil to turn black. Establishment to use 3 bay sink to sanitize. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2026

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (potato cutter) was observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC took unit out of service to be washed/sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2026

Various items were observed held between 108-120 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Staff reheated during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2026

Cut lettuce was observed held at 58 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC placed in cooler unit for active cooling. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2026

Various items were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Staff placed date during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2026
Inspector observed tuna salad past the 7 day date marking. Staff discarded tuna salad. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2026

Inspector observed in-use utensils being stored in a container with chemical sanitizer. Staff removed, washed/sanitized utensils, and placed in a container of hot water on the grill. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2026

Raw meats were observed thawing on the counter. PIC placed items in walk-in cooler. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must be thawed in one of the following manners: 1) under refrigeration (41 degrees Fahrenheit or less); 2) completely submerged under running water; 3) as part of the cooking process; or 4) in the microwave if cooked immediately after thawing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2026

The door to the outside, located in the back, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2024
Knife was observed stored in crevice of the prep unit hood. Staff removed and washed/sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2026
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the quat AND chlorine sanitizing solution for the 3 bay sinks. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2024
The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dish machine. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2026
The liquids from the keg machines and soda dispensing gun is draining into containers. Non-sewage liquids must drain into a drain. New Violation. Correct By: 04/23/2026
The receptacle in the women's toilet room is lacking a cover. A toilet room used by females shall be provided with a covered receptacle for sanitary napkins. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2024

The ceiling and light shield in the kitchen area was observed in poor repair (ceiling bowing and light shield cracked). The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 04/30/2026
The floor in the kitchen area is in poor repair (broken floor tiles). The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 10/31/2026

An employee was observed touching ready to eat food (sub roll) with bare hands. Food employees must not touch exposed, ready-to-eat food with their bare hands and shall use suitable utensils such as deli tissue, spatulas, single use gloves, or dispensing equipment. Disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2024

The handwashing sinks in restrooms are lacking handwashing signage. A sign or poster that notifies food employees to wash their hands shall be provided at all handwashing sinks. New Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2024

Raw animal foods (shelled eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (chicken wings) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2024

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was (0) parts per million (ppm).The dishwasher in kitchen was lacking chemical sanitizer for dishwasher machine. Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. New Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2024

The door to the outside, located in the (back hallway area), is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2024

Wood is used in the construction of (interior of walk in cooler ) in the (kitchen) area. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. New Violation. Correct By: 11/14/2024
Walk in cooler located in the (kitchen) area was not in good repair. Ambient temperatures observed at 44DF. New Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2024
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the (quaternary) sanitizing solution for the (3 bay sink ). New Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2024
The receptacle in the women's toilet room is lacking a cover. A toilet room used by females shall be provided with a covered receptacle for sanitary napkins. New Violation. Correct By: 08/14/2024
